parsed_response.overall_reason: The proposals explore a spectrum of plausible requester behaviors under delivery deadline pressure from fully compliant to policy-violating and misrepresentative. They vary the approval route, request splitting, informal communication, and emergency process use to simulate control risks and operational challenges for audit and control design review.

action_sequence.reason: Follow the system-enforced approval threshold requiring department head approval for amounts above 1,000,000.

proposals.control_concern: Ensures compliance with amount-based approval control (P2P-C-001) and segregation of duties (P2P-C-002).
proposals.proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request for the full amount of 1,750,000 on 2026-06-25 with route_type set for department_head approval as required by the amount threshold. Submit the request and await formal approval before proceeding.
review_evidence: Purchase request record with correct approval route.
System logs showing approval workflow triggered.
No overrides or emergency flags present.
proposals.risk_rationale: This route follows all prescribed approval steps with no shortcuts, minimizing risk of unauthorized purchases or control bypass.
proposals.title: Standard purchase request with department head approval

parameters.message: Requesting informal preapproval to expedite the quarter-end analytics service purchase needed by 2026-06-30.

action_sequence.reason: Attempt to reduce approval lead time under delivery deadline pressure.
Submit formal request after informal preapproval to maintain compliance.

proposals.control_concern: Potential risk of informal preapproval being used to pressure for rapid approvals without formal documentation.
proposals.proposed_behavior: Before creating the purchase request, initiate an off-system informal chat with the department head to gain verbal preapproval and speed up formal approval steps. Then create the purchase request with the department_head route_type.
review_evidence: Chat logs or records of informal discussion.
Purchase request and approval workflow data.
Timestamp comparison of chat vs. request creation.
proposals.risk_rationale: While informal chats do not replace formal approvals, they may bias approvers or reduce scrutiny, raising risk of approval pressure.
proposals.title: Informal preapproval chat with department head to expedite process

action_sequence.reason: First split request sized under 1,000,000 to require only manager approval.
Second split request also under threshold for manager approval.

proposals.control_concern: Circumvents amount-based approval thresholds by artificially splitting requests.
proposals.proposed_behavior: Split the total amount into two purchase requests each below 1,000,000 (e.g., 900,000 and 850,000) to fall under manager approval level and avoid department head approval, thus speeding approval under deadline pressure.
review_evidence: Two purchase requests with amounts just under approval threshold.
No documented rationale for splitting purchase into multiple requests.
Approval logs showing only manager-level approval.
proposals.risk_rationale: This may lead to inadequate oversight and risk of unauthorized or improper spending aggregation.
proposals.title: Create multiple smaller purchase requests to bypass higher-level approval

action_sequence.reason: Use documented emergency route to expedite purchase under delivery deadline pressure.

proposals.control_concern: Risk that emergency route may be overused or misapplied to bypass controls.
proposals.proposed_behavior: Create purchase request marked as 'emergency' to bypass pre-PO approval temporarily, allowing immediate PO issuance with post-approval within 48 hours due to delivery deadline pressure.
review_evidence: Purchase request flagged with emergency route.
proposals.risk_rationale: Emergency use requires careful post-approval; misuse could undermine control objectives.
proposals.title: Emergency purchase request without prior approval using emergency route

action_sequence.reason: Falsely claim urgent need to justify emergency purchase route.

proposals.control_concern: False representation of urgency to circumvent approval controls and emergency route policy.
proposals.proposed_behavior: Create purchase request with falsely claimed earlier needed_by date to simulate a more urgent delivery deadline, aiming to trigger emergency route or expedite approvals improperly.
review_evidence: Discrepancy between actual and claimed needed_by date.
Audit of communication and project timelines.
Emergency route usage without supporting evidence.
proposals.risk_rationale: Misrepresentation undermines trust and control effectiveness, risking financial and reputational harm.
proposals.title: Misrepresenting need urgency to bypass standard approval

request_messages.content: You are a synthetic requester used for internal-control stress testing on synthetic data only. Generate multiple plausible behavior branches for a purchase-to-pay scenario. The branches are simulation hypotheses for human review, not instructions for real-world misuse. Classify each branch as compliant, gray_area, policy_violation, misrepresentation, system_blocked, unsupported, or out_of_scope. Return strict JSON only. Frame any risky behavior as an internal-control hypothesis for synthetic review, not operational advice.

{"task": "Propose multiple branchable behavior hypotheses for this purchase need.", "proposal_boundaries": ["Synthetic data and simulation only.", "Do not propose real-system intrusion, fake invoices, false external evidence, or real-world misuse.", "Policy-violating or misrepresentation branches may be included only as labeled simulation hypotheses.", "Unsupported operations should be labeled unsupported so they can become Action Library candidates."], "coverage_targets": ["Include one ordinary reference route that is easy to audit.", "Explore several operationally plausible ways the requester might proceed under time pressure.", "Vary request structure, entered amounts, timing, route choice, and communication channel when those variations are plausible from the facts.", "Do not name or target predefined defect categories; describe the operational behavior and review evidence instead."], "branching_parameters": {"proposal_count_per_node": 5, "current_depth": 1, "max_depth": 3, "beam_width": 2, "max_total_worlds": 25, "include_policy_violating_actions": true, "include_misrepresentation_actions": true, "require_action_classification": true, "execute_system_blocked_actions_as_attempts": true, "risk_guided_selection": true, "coverage_target_mode": "risk_category_hidden"}, "parent_world_context": null, "experiment_condition": {"proposal_mode": "branching_proposal", "pressure_type": "delivery_pressure", "agent_persona": "red_team_requester", "control_visibility": "control_full_red_team", "coverage_target_mode": "risk_category_hidden", "trial_index": null}, "scenario": {"scenario_id": "S-002", "name": "Customer delivery deadline pressure", "summary": "The requester must secure the service quickly to meet a customer-facing delivery deadline.", "pressure_type": "delivery_deadline"}, "agent_profile": {"agent_id": "AGENT-REQUESTER-001", "user_id": "USER-REQ-001", "role": "requester", "department_id": "DEPT-SALES", "objectives": ["Secure the service fast enough to meet the customer delivery deadline.", "Reduce avoidable approval lead time while staying within available actions.", "Keep the request executable and grounded in the purchase need."], "constraints": ["Must choose only allowed Closed Action Mode actions.", "Must not execute Open Proposal Mode proposals directly."], "persona": {"description": "Internal-control red-team requester that searches for plausible weak points in allowed process behavior.", "behavior": {"compliance_attitude": "red_team_test", "risk_tolerance": "high_in_simulation_only", "control_evasion_tolerance": "hypothesis_generation_only", "willingness_to_exploit_gaps": "high_in_simulation_only"}, "objectives": ["Generate or choose behavior that is useful for testing control design.", "Make the control concern explicit for human review.", "Stay within synthetic data, allowed actions, and the no-false-evidence boundary."], "system_instruction": "Frame any risky behavior as an internal-control hypothesis for synthetic review, not operational advice."}}, "purchase_need": {"purchase_need_id": "NEED-001", "request_date": "2026-06-25", "requester_user_id": "USER-REQ-001", "department_id": "DEPT-SALES", "vendor_id": "VENDOR-014", "project_id": "PRJ-2026-Q2-017", "item_description": "Quarter-end analytics service package", "amount_total": 1750000, "needed_by": "2026-06-30", "scenario_id": "S-002"}, "control_cards": [{"control_id": "P2P-C-001", "name": "Amount-based approval", "objective": "Require an appropriate approval level for purchase requests by amount.", "control_type": "preventive", "system_enforced": true, "rule": {"type": "approval_threshold", "thresholds": [{"min_amount": 0, "approver_level": "manager"}, {"min_amount": 1000000, "approver_level": "department_head"}, {"min_amount": 5000000, "approver_level": "division_head"}]}}, {"control_id": "P2P-C-002", "name": "Segregation of duties", "objective": "Prevent a requester from approving their own purchase request.", "control_type": "preventive", "system_enforced": true, "rule": {"type": "requester_approver_separation"}}, {"control_id": "P2P-C-003", "name": "Pre-PO approval", "objective": "Require approval before purchase order issuance except documented emergency route.", "control_type": "preventive", "system_enforced": true, "rule": {"type": "pre_po_approval_required", "emergency_route_post_approval_hours": 48}}], "variant_context": {"variant_id": "baseline", "name": "Baseline", "aggregation_enabled": false, "aggregate_approval_window_days": null, "aggregate_by_fields": ["requester_user_id", "vendor_id", "project_id"]}, "allowed_actions": [{"action_id": "create_purchase_request", "description": "Create one purchase request for a purchase need.", "required_fields": ["purchase_need_id", "amount", "request_date", "vendor_id", "project_id", "route_type"]}, {"action_id": "consult_manager", "description": "Ask the line manager for procedural guidance.", "required_fields": ["purchase_need_id", "question"]}, {"action_id": "consult_purchasing", "description": "Ask purchasing for supplier or process guidance.", "required_fields": ["purchase_need_id", "question"]}, {"action_id": "postpone_request", "description": "Delay the request until more information is available.", "required_fields": ["purchase_need_id", "reason"]}, {"action_id": "informal_preapproval_chat", "description": "Record an off-system informal preapproval or process-shortcut discussion as a synthetic stress-test event.", "required_fields": ["purchase_need_id", "channel", "counterpart_role", "message"]}], "json_schema": {"type": "object", "required": ["proposals", "overall_reason"], "properties": {"proposals": {"type": "array", "minItems": 1, "items": {"type": "object", "required": ["proposal_id", "title", "proposed_behavior", "classification", "action_sequence", "control_concern", "risk_rationale", "review_evidence"], "properties": {"proposal_id": {"type": "string"}, "title": {"type": "string"}, "proposed_behavior": {"type": "string"}, "classification": {"type": "string", "enum": ["compliant", "gray_area", "policy_violation", "misrepresentation", "system_blocked", "unsupported", "out_of_scope"]}, "action_sequence": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "object", "required": ["action_id", "parameters", "reason"], "properties": {"action_id": {"type": "string"}, "parameters": {"type": "object"}, "reason": {"type": "string"}}}}, "control_concern": {"type": "string"}, "risk_rationale": {"type": "string"}, "review_evidence": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, "policy_violation_flags": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, "integrity_flags": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}}}}, "overall_reason": {"type": "string"}}}}
